CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value VARCHAR);
SELECT either(id, value, 'not specified') FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT either(mod(id, 2), 'odd', 'even') AS parity FROM test_table;
SELECT either((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value = 'odd'), 'odd exists', 'odd not found') AS odd_check;
SELECT either((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table), 'has entries', 'empty table') AS table_status;
SELECT either((SELECT SUM(id) FROM test_table), 0, 'sum is zero') AS sum_result;
SELECT either(id, (SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE id = 2), 'id not found') FROM test_table WHERE id = 3;
SELECT either(CASE WHEN id > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END, 'greater than one', 'one or less') FROM test_table;
SELECT either(NULL, 'default value', 'fallback') AS null_test FROM test_table WHERE id = 0;
SELECT either(id, 'id is zero', 'not zero') FROM test_table WHERE id = 0;
SELECT either((SELECT MIN(id) FROM test_table), 'no minimum', 'minimum found') AS min_result FROM test_table;
SELECT either(mod(id, 3), 'divisible by 3', 'not divisible by 3') AS divisibility FROM test_table;
SELECT either(isnull((SELECT value FROM test_table WHERE id = 3)), 'value missing', 'value present') AS value_check;
